summary: The test suite provides unit-level coverage of core parsing and version bumping logic (parseVersion, determineBumpType, bumpVersion, parseConventionalCommit, generateChangelog) but has critical gaps: (1) writeVersionToPackageJson function is completely untested despite being in the implementation, (2) the entire main.ts CLI module (~90 lines) is untested—no tests for argument parsing, file I/O, or the main workflow, (3) no integration tests exercising the full pipeline, (4) edge cases like scoped commits (feat(scope):), malformed BREAKING CHANGE footers, and error scenarios (missing files, invalid JSON) are largely unexercised. Design is solid with clear test organization and naming, but rigor is weak—mostly happy-path testing with minimal boundary condition exploration. Approximately 50% of the implementation code is untested. This suite would catch regressions in parsing logic but would miss failures in the CLI interface, file operations, and integration.
